title: amelanchier ovalis subsp. integrifolia
intro: Amelanchier ovalis subsp. integrifolia, also known as the oval-leaved shad or Canadian serviceberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and showy white flowers that bloom in spring. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Amelanchier ovalis subsp. integrifolia include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in natural remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
